---
title: "Run differential expression"
description: "Pseudobulk differential expression across conditions with DESeq2."
categories: [openpipeline]
order: 50
---

[`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline}<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>

Once cells are [annotated](annotate.qmd), you can test which genes change between
conditions (genotype, treatment, disease vs. control). OpenPipeline uses a
**pseudobulk** approach: within each cell type, counts are summed per sample into
bulk-like samples that DESeq2 then models, so the unit of replication is the
sample, not the cell, avoiding the inflated significance of treating thousands of
cells from one donor as independent.

## Options

| Workflow | Use when |
|---|---|
| [`differential_expression/pseudobulk_deseq2`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/differential_expression/pseudobulk_deseq2.qmd) | Compare gene expression between conditions, per cell type |

## Example: genotype contrast

For example, comparing wildtype vs. mutant per cell type:

```yaml
# params.yaml
id: pbmc_de
input: results/annotated.h5mu
obs_cell_group: cell_type          # test within each cell type
obs_groups: [genotype, sample_id]  # columns that define a pseudobulk sample
design_formula: "~ genotype"
contrast_column: genotype
contrast_values: [wildtype, mutant]
publish_dir: results/
```

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline.git \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/differential_expression/pseudobulk_deseq2/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

## Key choices

- **`obs_cell_group`**: the `.obs` cell-type column; DE runs within each group.
- **`obs_groups`**: the columns that define a pseudobulk sample (condition +
  sample).
- **`design_formula` / `contrast_column` / `contrast_values`**: the DESeq2 model
  and the two levels compared.
- A group needs **at least two samples per condition** to be testable, so rare
  cell types may be dropped.

## Output

Per-cell-type differential-expression tables (log fold changes, p-values,
adjusted p-values) written to the publish directory.

:::{.callout-tip}
Per-sample vs. shared arguments and file formats are covered in
[Process many samples](process-many-samples.qmd).
:::
